stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Stan is the nickname of a dinosaur fossil specimen housed at the Abu Dhabi Natural History Museum.
  • The Abu Dhabi Natural History Museum is located in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates.
  • Stan is a member of the genus Tyrannosaurus.
  • Trix is the nickname of a dinosaur fossil specimen housed at Naturalis Biodiversity Center.
  • Naturalis Biodiversity Center is located in Leiden, Netherlands.
  • Trix is a member of the genus Tyrannosaurus.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The dinosaur fossil specimens Stan and Trix are both members of the genus Tyrannosaurus.
The Abu Dhabi Natural History Museum, where Stan is housed, is located in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates.
Trix is the nickname of a Tyrannosaurus fossil specimen housed at Naturalis Biodiversity Center in Leiden, Netherlands.
While Stan is housed at the Abu Dhabi Natural History Museum, Trix is housed at Naturalis Biodiversity Center.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity between the two specimens, noting that Stan and Trix are members of the same genus: Tyrannosaurus.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence only provides information about where one of the specimens (Stan) can be found; it doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence only provides information about one specimen (Trix); it doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two specimens. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence contrasts the locations of Stan and Trix; it doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two specimens.
